response.justification: The role's minimum bar is 8 years of C++ and 5 years building large-scale infrastructure/distributed systems, with technical leadership of a cloud compute platform. This candidate is an accomplished generalist (TypeScript/Node, DLang, web/mobile/embedded) with real distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby and strong mentoring/teaching credentials, but C++ appears in only one ~6-month role at National Instruments plus a thesis project — far short of 8 years. There is no evidence of leading an internal cloud compute platform or production cloud architecture. The gap on the core hard requirements is too large to advance.
strengths: Distributed/low-latency systems experience handling millions of requests per second
Strong mentoring, teaching, and engineering-practice background (TDD, workshops, dojos)
Broad, adaptable full-stack engineering across many domains
concerns: C++ experience is roughly 6 months, well below the 8-year minimum
No internal cloud compute platform or production cloud architecture experience
No formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: Cloud compute platform / cloud architecture experience
